# [CHECKLIST TYPE] Checklist: [EXISTING SLICE NAME]

**Purpose**: [Brief description of what this checklist covers]
**Created**: [DATE]
**Slice specification**: [Link to spec.md or relevant documentation]

## [Category 1]

- [ ] CHK001 First checklist item with clear action
- [ ] CHK002 Second checklist item
- [ ] CHK003 Third checklist item

## [Category 2]

- [ ] CHK004 Another category item
- [ ] CHK005 Item with specific criteria
- [ ] CHK006 Final item in this category

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Reject implementation readiness unless
  `python3 scripts/run_slice_workflow.py run <nunchi-plan|speckit>
  specs/<exact-slice>` binds and preflights that existing slice without
  persisting feature state, resume occurs only by run ID, the one valid
  `evidence/governance/v2-implementation-authorization.md` enumerates exactly
  slices `010` through `110`, and the bound slice's independent
  owner/dependency/analysis/worktree/activation-evidence gate passes
- Reject any authorization record that does not enumerate exactly all eleven
  slices `010` through `110`; partial scope leaves every slice dormant
- Confirm assignment came from Zoe or a durably named delegate and its stable
  reference names assignee, exact slice/owner lane, assigner, and ISO date;
  assignment neither grants authority nor requires unrelated slices to be staffed
- For dependent slices, require ordered `slice=full-sha` dependency commits and
  matching `slice=repo-relative-evidence-reference` per-consumer acceptances;
  slice `110` requires every upstream slice to be `ACCEPTED`
- On rework, require an append-only `REJECTED` handoff record followed by a new
  bound delivery run and later candidate/handoff attempt; reject resume of the
  completed run, deleted/rewritten history, or resume after convergence changed
  the task graph. Permit resume only for a paused post-convergence fix with an
  unchanged task graph
- Reject a central mutable slice-status registry or integration/cutover work
  outside slice `110`
- Reject a bare `NO_IMPACT`, a generic "update docs" item, or a `HANDOFF`
  without an exact delta and accepting owner
- Reject a directory wildcard when the affected documentation files are known
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
